what_it_does: Defines the motion language under the rule 'nothing teleports': duration and easing tokens, travel-distance and stagger tokens, ENTER/EXIT keyframes (cvEnter*/cvExit* in four directions plus scale) with matching .enter-*/.exit-* classes, a .stagger child-delay rule, a .moves transition for FLIP/View-Transitions repositioning, ::view-transition styling, and a prefers-reduced-motion override.
observations: Durations: instant 90ms, quick 160ms, move 280ms, enter 360ms, exit 240ms, page 480ms
Travel distances --move-xs..lg plus --move-off 120% (fully off frame); stagger step 55ms, max 10
Enter keyframes fade+translate in from a direction (Up/Down/Left/Right) plus cvEnterScale; exit keyframes mirror outward
Enter classes use --ease-entrance, exit classes use --ease-exit, both with animation-fill 'both'
.moves transitions transform+opacity over --dur-move with --ease-emphasized for FLIP/VT repositioning
::view-transition-group(*) duration set to --dur-page; root old/new use --ease-emphasized
prefers-reduced-motion collapses all animation/transition durations to 1ms (content still appears)
Header notes durations extend dur-fast/base/slow from colors_and_type.css
standouts: Three-primitive model (ENTER/EXIT/MOVE) framed as 'nothing teleports' — a stated motion philosophy
First-class support for the View Transitions API alongside a FLIP fallback, both routed through the same tokens
Reduced-motion handled by collapsing durations to 1ms rather than removing animations, so 'both' fill still applies end state
conventions: Token-then-rule pattern; all 'feel' values are tokens tunable in one place
@kind other annotations on duration/easing/stagger non-length tokens
Accessibility gate (prefers-reduced-motion) included as a first-class concern
Direction suffix convention documented (where it comes FROM / goes TO)
notes: The single home for the design system's motion feel — durations, easings, enter/exit/move primitives, stagger, View-Transitions/FLIP support, and reduced-motion; add .enter-* on mount, .exit-* before unmount.
questions: Where are --dur-fast/base/slow (extended per the header) defined in colors_and_type.css?
The FLIP fallback and the JS that adds/removes .enter-*/.exit-* and wraps startViewTransition are referenced as a 'helper component' — where does that live?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header declares the 'nothing teleports' three-primitive motion language; the file delivers enter/exit/move primitives, tokens, stagger, VT support, and reduced-motion gating.
apparent_themes: View Transitions / FLIP for layout moves
intention_signals: Trying to make every state/page change a choreographed travel rather than a flash, with the entire feel tunable from tokens and degraded gracefully for reduced-motion users.
novelty: The explicit ENTER/EXIT/MOVE triad as a named philosophy, and treating View Transitions and FLIP as two mechanisms of one MOVE primitive driven by shared tokens.
contribution: Contributes the motion token set and the enter/exit/move animation primitives plus stagger, View-Transitions styling, and the reduced-motion gate — the system's choreography layer.
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV design-system CSS defining motion tokens (durations, easings, travel, stagger) and ENTER/EXIT/MOVE animation primitives with stagger, View-Transitions support, and a prefers-reduced-motion gate.
intention_for_embedding: To make every state and page change a choreographed exit+entrance ('nothing teleports'), tunable from single-source tokens and respectful of reduced-motion.
